mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative 5-day returns in more than 65% of observations during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate coinciding with an increasing unemployment rate creates a stagflationary monetary tightening environment that suppresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 50% of periods where both FEDFUNDS and UNRATE rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth surpassing 2.0% quarter-over-quarter confirms robust aggregate demand expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth exceeding 2.0% is followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding employment and wage growth, which directly supports corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions yield negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising consumer price index values indicates supply-side bottlenecks and cost-push inflation, which compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases.
